QF.id: XIII Trading, Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_38

question.text: In the clearing member collateral utilization framework, what happens when a clearing member's utilization reaches 90%?

answer.text: The clearing member enters risk reduction mode at 90% utilization, where the exchange imposes restrictions on taking new positions and may require additional collateral
The clearing member is immediately suspended from trading and all positions are liquidated
The clearing member receives a warning but can continue trading normally until utilization reaches 100%
The clearing member's margin requirements are reduced by 10% to bring utilization below the threshold

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When a clearing member's collateral utilization reaches 90%, the exchange activates risk reduction mode. In this mode, only offsetting trades (reducing positions) are permitted for the CM's clients. For proprietary positions, the threshold may be 85%. Additional margin/collateral is requested immediately. **The Trap:** Risk reduction mode does NOT mean immediate suspension or position liquidation. It restricts new position build-up while allowing square-offs. The 90% and 85% thresholds apply to different categories (client vs proprietary). **Cross-Exam Context:** Risk reduction mode is distinct from the MWPL ban (95% of MWPL). The former is CM-specific based on collateral utilization; the latter is stock-specific based on aggregate OI. Both restrict new positions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** CMs must actively monitor utilization. Risk reduction mode can cascade — if a CM enters risk reduction, all TMs clearing through it are affected, even those individually well-capitalized.
